operating_state: Broadcom generated record revenue of $27.5 billion, increasing 15% driven by semiconductor solutions growth from higher wireless product demand and mobile handset production recovery, while infrastructure software grew from increased FC SAN, mainframe and cyber security solutions demand. Customer concentration remained significant with top five end customers representing over 35% of revenue and Apple approximately 20%. Operating cash flow reached $13.8 billion, increasing $1.7 billion from higher net income partially offset by unfavorable working capital changes.
cost_structure: Gross margin expanded to 61% from 57% primarily due to lower acquisition-related intangible asset amortization and favorable semiconductor margins from supply imbalance conditions creating higher average selling prices. Operating expenses declined from prior year Symantec acquisition costs and completed integration benefits. Manufacturing costs increased from supply chain disruptions with suppliers extending lead times and raising prices particularly for wafers and substrates.
material_events: Company paid $6.2 billion in cash dividends and completed $5.5 billion debt exchange in March 2021, swapping notes maturing 2024-2027 for longer-term notes due 2033-2034. Board authorized $10 billion stock repurchase program effective through December 2022 and declared quarterly dividends of $4.10 per common share. California Institute of Technology awarded $270.2 million patent infringement judgment currently under appeal while Huawei sales suspended due to U.S. export restrictions requiring Department of Commerce licenses.
macro_exposures: Escalating U.S.-China trade tensions resulted in export restrictions and increased tariffs with approximately 35% of revenue shipping to China though end customer locations frequently differ. COVID-19 pandemic disrupted global supply chain with Malaysia warehouse operational while key suppliers experienced shutdowns affecting wafer and substrate availability. Korean Fair Trade Commission investigating contracting practices with growing anti-competition regulatory scrutiny alongside foreign exchange rate fluctuation risks despite hedging strategies.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued supply chain challenges with demand outpacing supply requiring ongoing build-to-order approach and extended lead times. Singapore tax incentives expire November 2025 and Malaysia tax holiday expires fiscal 2028, creating future tax rate increases. Company anticipates semiconductor industry cyclicality with current supply imbalance and record profitability potentially unsustainable longer-term while current cash and operating cash flows provide sufficient liquidity for operations and acquisitions.
uncertainty_profile: Pending appeal of California Institute of Technology patent infringement judgment with potential significant damages and Apple indemnification obligations alongside Korean Fair Trade Commission investigation outcome uncertainty. Transfer pricing audits across multiple jurisdictions could result in material tax adjustments including potential deduction reductions and global minimum tax initiatives. COVID-19 pandemic duration and variants impact remains unpredictable affecting supply chain recovery with effects unlikely fully realized until future periods.
